Introduction to the History of Guatemala
Guatemala has been producing coffee since 1850.

Around 97% of Guatemala's coffee production is Arabica, 
mostly grown at elevations above 1,000 meters above sea level.

We started in 2012 when, during PhD studies, there was a 
need to receive coffee samples for research purposes.

Today, we supply more than 46 types of coffee to a wide 
range of industries.
We offer regional and commercial coffees.

Available processing methods include Natural, Washed, Honey 
and Anaerobic.
We work with traditional varieties, exotic varieties, and nano lots.
